title: Cyathea andohahelensis
intro: Cyathea aohahelensis, also known as the "Madagascar Tree Fern", is a species of tree fern native to the island of Madagascar. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Cyathea aohahelensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Cyathea aohahelensis is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or powder, and is also used in the production of herbal supplements and topical creams.
taxonomy: Cyathea andohahelensis is a species of tree fern in the family Cyatheaceae. It is native to Madagascar. Cyathea andohahelensis belongs to the phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Cyatheales, and family Cyatheaceae. This classification places it among the true ferns. Further, it is categorized within the genus Cyathea, which comprises various tree fern species.
uses: Cyathea andohahelensis is a species of tree fern that is native to Madagascar. While there is limited information available on the medicinal uses of Cyathea andohahelensis, it is known to be used in traditional medicine in some parts of its native range. The plant's rhizome and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses.
